basic_info.incident_name: Synthron Runaway Chemical Reaction and Vapor Cloud Explosion
basic_info.incident_name_cn: 辛索恩公司失控化学反应及蒸气云爆炸事故

process_context.operating_conditions: Batch polymerization at elevated temperature; scaled-up charge without reassessment of thermal balance or cooling capacity

failure_mechanism.root_cause: Scaling up the monomer charge without evaluating thermal accumulation and cooling capacity led to excessive heat generation exceeding condenser removal capability.
failure_mechanism.causal_chain: Recipe scaling → excess monomer in initial charge → increased exothermic reaction rate → heat generation > condenser removal capacity → reactor temperature and pressure rise → solvent vapor venting via manway → flammable vapor cloud formation → ignition → explosion
failure_mechanism.physical_principles: Exothermic polymerization kinetics, heat transfer limitation, vapor pressure–temperature relationship, deflagration of stoichiometric hydrocarbon-air mixture

existing_safeguards: Visual monitoring via condenser sight glass
protection_system.safeguard_failures: No independent thermal hazard review; no high-temperature/high-pressure alarms; no automatic emergency cooling activation; no relief system designed for runaway scenario; reliance on operator observation only
csb_recommendations: Conduct rigorous reactive hazard assessments (e.g., RC1, ARC, DSC) for all processes involving energetic chemistry
Implement automated process safety interlocks including temperature/pressure trip to emergency cooling and reactor isolation
Establish and enforce management of change (MOC) procedures for any recipe or operating parameter modification

embedding_text: At Synthron’s NC facility, a 1500-gallon batch reactor experienced runaway polymerization of acrylic monomers due to unassessed recipe scaling. Excess initial monomer caused more rapid exothermic energy release than the condenser could remove, leading to temperature and pressure escalation. Solvent vapors vented through the manway, forming an ignitable vapor cloud inside the building. Ignition triggered a vapor cloud explosion, killing one worker and destroying the facility. No thermal hazard analysis or automated safeguards were in place.
